title: Composite-leaf givenness: expandCompositeInstance marks leaf-model DEFAULT params as given (setModelParam) -> use markGiven:false. Agents flagged optocoupler.ts:54 and the diac diode.ts:913 0-step failures as the SAME cause (verify by re-run after the compiler fix).
fixHint: Do not mark leaf-default model params as given during composite expansion. Use a non-given default-seeding path so isModelParamGiven only reports params explicitly set in the netlist or by the user, matching standalone placement.
diagnosis: Composite expansion copies every leaf-model DEFAULT param into subProps via setModelParam, which marks it as user-given. For the phototransistor BJT spice model this marks RCO (default 0.01) as given, so bjt.ts:1549 rcoGiven becomes true and the BJT setup splits off the Kull quasi-sat colPrime internal node (bjt.ts:1756). ngspice deck has no RCO so it splits no node. Result is an extra MNA equation (12 vs 11), an orphan node tx:bjtPhoto:collector, a singular subsystem, non-convergence, and NaN node voltages so converged stays false.

title: Drivers stamp rail-level vTarget onto ctrl_out but DigitalOutputPinLoaded re-applies rail span -> stamp normalized [0,1] level
fixHint: The driver must stamp the NORMALIZED logic level onto ctrl_out, not the rail-level vTarget. With latch=0 inactive the logic level is 1 (-> outPin drives vOH) and latch=1 active is 0 (-> vOL), so stamp (1 - wForStamp) as the ctrl target at line 193, letting the outPins eDrive VCVS do the single rail mapping.
diagnosis: ComparatorDriver stamps a RAIL-LEVEL Thevenin target vTarget = (1-w)*vOH + w*vOL onto the internal ctrl_out net, but the downstream DigitalOutputPinLoaded.outPin expects ctrl in NORMALIZED [0,1] V (digital-output-pin-loaded.ts:8-11) and re-applies the rail mapping via its eDrive VCVS gain=(vOH-vOL). The rail span is applied twice, so ctrl_out near vOH=5V is multiplied again, driving cmp:out far over rail (15.87V instead of 3.3V).

title: Inverted comparator latch polarity (sink when V+>V- instead of V+<V-)

fixHint: Invert the latch transition conditions at lines 165-166: latch should go to 1 (sinking) when vPlus < vTl and release to 0 when vPlus >= vTh, so V+>V- yields not-sinking (HIGH) and V+<V- yields sinking (LOW).
diagnosis: Open-collector comparator latch polarity is inverted: latch=1 (active/sinking, drives vOL/LOW) is set when vPlus >= vTh i.e. when in+ is ABOVE in-, but a comparator drives output HIGH when V+ > V-. So in+ above in- wrongly sinks the output low instead of letting the pull-up hold it high.

fixHint: In the transient gain-stage the vos offset must propagate through the same loop gain as the steady-state DC path; the line-555 RHS uses aEff (bandwidth-reduced) for vos rather than the integrator-mediated full offset, so the 5-step transient under-realizes the offset. Either drive to DCOP or carry vos through vInt (line 454) consistently into the output without the aEff attenuation at 555.
diagnosis: Test sets vos=0.5 then steps 5 times (transient) and expects unity-follower Vout to shift by ~0.5V; actual shift is only 0.155V. In transient (geq_int>0) the gain-stage normal branch uses aEff=aol/(1+g) (line 459) which is a heavily attenuated effective gain, and the vos contribution to the output RHS at line 555 is aEffScaled*G_out*p.vos, so the offset is amplified by the reduced aEff rather than the full open-loop aol. After only 5 small steps the integrator (vInt) has not slewed enough to realize the full 0.5V offset, and the transient vos RHS term uses the bandwidth-reduced aEff. The closed-loop offset is therefore far below 0.5V.

At step1/iter0 the only difference is the RHS internal-node row: ours=1 ngspice=0.9999999999999999 which propagates to node TL1:int2 ours=1 vs 0.9999999999999999 and node R_LOAD:pos ours=0.5 vs 0.49999999999999994. The TRA load seeds _input1/_input2 from rhsOld (line 268-269) so the step-0 DC-OP RHS accumulation order (MODEDC bridge stamps lines 244-256 plus resistor) is where the 1-ULP first enters; the matrix being bit-identical means per the Sparse-Solver-Settled directive the cause is load/RHS accumulation order not the solver.

fixHint: The invariant cannot be met through the C*vcap path while node voltages carry 1-ULP solver roundoff between identical steps. Either the recompute must reuse the rotated charge when the converged voltage is unchanged (predictor/INITPRED-style copy s1->s0) so state0 is bit-identical, or the strict slot-equality invariant must be re-derived against what ngspice actually guarantees at steady state. Escalate the disposition.
diagnosis: After an accepted DC-steady-state step the test requires state0[C1.Q]===state1[C1.Q] strictly (Object.is). The capacitor recomputes its charge each NR iteration as s0[Q]=C*vcap where vcap=v0-v1 is the freshly NR-solved terminal voltage (line 582). state1[Q] is the rotated copy of the prior step charge. The two NR solves of the identical steady-state system settle on node voltages that differ by 1 ULP so C*vcap yields a Q 1 ULP off (4.999999999999998e-6 vs 4.9999999999999996e-6); strict equality fails. The divergence is in the live-voltage charge recompute, not the rotation.

fixHint: This is correct SPICE-parity caching - the slot is meant to lag by one iteration. After the last converged iteration the cached slot therefore holds the second-to-last solutions bias point, one NR step (0.73mV) behind engine.getNodeVoltage(V1:P). This lag is intrinsic SPICE behavior (state slots cache the bias used for linearization, not the post-solve node difference), matching ngspice *vpk=vpk semantics.

fixHint: In DC mode the memristor must read the last-accepted W from s1 (like the inductor), not s0. Reading s0 only holds the seed on the FIRST load before any rotation; after a warm-start rotation the valid value is in s1. Resolve W from s1 (or seed s1 too) for the DC branch at line 226.
diagnosis: After buildFixture warm-start (one transient step) the last-accepted W lives in s1 because rotateStateVectors at the top of the next step recycles states[0]. The second dcOperatingPoint() then re-enters DC mode where load() reads wOld = s0[W] (line 226), which is the recycled/stale buffer not the valid last-accepted W, so DCOP linearizes around a wrong W (driving R toward rOn) and V(mem:neg)=0.834 instead of the w=0.5 value 0.1105.

fixHint: Either the rejection path must restore state pool s0 from s1 before the retry to honor the asserted invariant, or the test invariant is wrong (ngspice intentionally does not restore state on LTE reject). Escalate: this is a state-rotation-on-reject semantics decision, not a crystal-component bug.
diagnosis: On LTE rejection the engine (analog-engine.ts:625-636) only rewinds simTime and sets dt=newDt; it deliberately does NOT restore the state pool (per the ngspice dctran.c:935-954 comment at line 626-629). So after a rejected step the NR-written s0 differs from the last-accepted s1 on the crystal leaf reactive slots (PHI/Q), giving -5.14e-8 vs -3.22e-8. The test asserts s0==s1 (rollback invariant) which the engine never establishes on the LTE-reject path.

fixHint: Test-precondition gap, not a source bug: shouldReject and cktTerr match ngspice. To exercise the rollback path the fixture needs sharper flux gradients or smaller minTimeStep so worstRatio exceeds 1.111, or the test must not assume a rejection always occurs. Escalate if a rejection is required by spec.
diagnosis: The test asserts an LTE rejection MUST occur for the square-wave RL (L=1e-3,R=1,500Hz,5ms,free-running maxStep), then reads it. No step in the convergence log has lteRejected===true so the lookup returns undefined and toBeDefined fails. shouldReject (timestep.ts:616, reject when worstRatio>=1/0.9) is ngspice-faithful; cktTerr never produced a ratio over 1.111 on this circuit so no rejection fired. The fixture params simply do not drive the LTE controller past the reject threshold.

fixHint: Make the transient first-step seeding (currentDt=firstStep and _seedFromDcop) robust to the transformer coupled-inductor DCOP path so MODEINITTRAN and a nonzero first dt are established; root the upstream DCOP convergence for the singular coupled-inductor branch block so result.converged is true after source-sweep as it is in ngspice.
diagnosis: Harness on transformer-canon-ac-step-down.dts shows our engine runs only 1 step then stalls (lastDt=0, did not advance) while ngspice runs 1009 steps. Our DCOP limps through 11 gminDynamic + 11 srcSweep attempts ALL accepted=false and we emit NO tranInit phase (ngspice emits 2 tranInit + tranPredictor/tranNR loop). The transient first-step dt seed (_timestep.currentDt = firstStep) and _seedFromDcop MODEINITTRAN seeding at line 1083-1091 are GATED on result.converged; for the coupled-inductor transformer the DCOP does not return a clean converged op (two inductors shorted at DC + mutual coupling form a singular branch block), so currentDt stays at the line-1061 seed value of 0 and getClampedDt returns ~0 at step() line 351, collapsing the first timestep. Matrix divergence confirms our inductor branch diagonal stays at DC req=0 where ngspice has the transient companion -50000.

fixHint: Make the diode setParam(BV,...) path re-establish the breakdown regime: ensure the finite-BV branch flags/seed used at setup (and the warm-start junction voltage) are refreshed so the next solve re-enters the breakdown arm, matching the compile-time BV=5 result. Verify configure({}) re-runs setup or that load() reads the live params.BV/tBV rather than a setup-captured copy. (Test should also observe the anode, not the source-pinned cathode.)
diagnosis: BV hot-load does not propagate into the diode breakdown behavior. MCP evidence: building the same reverse-bias circuit with BV=5 at compile time gives anode=4.958V (breakdown engages); building with default BV=Inf then circuit_patch set BV=5 keeps anode=0.000V (breakdown never engages) even though setComponentProperty routes to el.setParam (coordinator.ts:916-917) which sets params.BV=5 and recomputes tBV via computeTemperature. The breakdown arm in load() therefore stays inactive after hot-load. Separately the test reads d1:K (cathode) which is hard-tied to the 10V source and can never change, so even a working BV change would be invisible at that node - but the underlying source bug is the BV hot-load not engaging breakdown (observable at the anode).

fixHint: In capacitor.ts setParam, after setting _nominalC recompute this.C = _nominalC * _SCALE (or have the engine re-run computeTemperature on any param change) so capacitance is truly hot-loadable.
diagnosis: Capacitor.setParam(capacitance) updates only _nominalC; the stamped capacitance this.C is recomputed solely in computeTemperature() (capacitor.ts:340/432), which a bare coordinator.step() after setComponentProperty never invokes. So the cBody stamp keeps the OLD capacitance and the post-hotload first-step current is identical (diff exactly 0), failing not.toBeCloseTo. Capacitance hot-load is inert until a temperature recompute fires. (Test also wrongly assumes first-step current is C-sensitive when it is esr-limited at tiny dt.)

fixHint: Read the ESR-limited current off cap:rEsr (the resistor in the series path), not the cap:cBody companion current, and read it at the first transient step before charging dominates.
diagnosis: Test reads getElementPinCurrents on cap:cBody (the capacitor body) expecting it to track V/esr. esr does hot-load correctly (Resistor.setParam calls updateConduct at resistor.ts:595), but the cBody companion current (CCAP) only equals V/esr at the very first transient step when the cap is a near-short. After the warm-start step plus one more step() the cap is already charging, so cBody CCAP is no longer V/esr and stays ~2 rather than dropping to ~1. The assertion observes the wrong leaf/quantity.

fixHint: Align the per-leaf load() walk order for the PolarizedCap composite so rEsr/rLeak/cBody stamp the nCap diagonal in the same sequence ngspice loads them (RESload before CAPload); verify expandCompositeInstance/ngspiceLoadOrder leaf ordering matches the emitted SPICE deck.
diagnosis: harness_first_divergence earliest = matrix cell (nCap,nCap) at step0/iter0, classification value-only, single cell, absDelta=1.78e-15 (1 ULP). The nCap diagonal accumulates three += stamps: rEsr.G + rLeak.G + cBody.geq. The leaf load order our engine walks differs in associativity from ngspice (R,R load then C load) for this expanded composite, producing a 1-ULP sum on the shared diagonal. Per CLAUDE.md this is the per-device load() accumulation order on a shared diagonal upstream cause, not the sparse solver. The 1.1e-16 node-voltage delta is the downstream symptom.

fixHint: Make _recordLimit emit the junction name VD (matching the DIOvoltage state-slot name at diode.ts:68 and ngspice diodefs.h:196) instead of the literal AK, so limiting events are identified by the same junction label the rest of the diode model uses.
diagnosis: events.length>=1 passes (line 573); the failing assertion is line 574 .some(e.junction===VD || e.junction.startsWith(V)). The diode _recordLimit hardcodes junction:AK (diode.ts:1258), an anode-cathode label that neither equals VD nor starts with V, so .some() returns false. The diode junction state slot is named VD (DIOvoltage, diodefs.h:196, declared at diode.ts:68) but the limiting record uses AK, an inconsistent junction identifier.

fixHint: Confirm via convergence log whether the engine errors on this fixture; if it errors, ensure the error-exit path records a StepRecord so the log is non-empty, or relax the fixture stiffness. If it does not error, audit why accepted steps skip the line-681 record after a post-warm-start setConvergenceLogEnabled(true).
diagnosis: Test enables the convergence log via coordinator.setConvergenceLogEnabled(true) (coordinator.ts:482-491) AFTER buildFixture warm-started with logging off, then runs 200 coordinator.step() calls; getConvergenceLog() returns a non-null but EMPTY array (length 0), failing log.length>=1 at line 623. A step records exactly one StepRecord at analog-engine.ts:681 when logging is on and the step is accepted. Empty after 200 steps means the transient body never reached the record (likely the engine hit EngineState.ERROR on the stiff 10V/100uF/esr=0.01 uic fixture, after which step() returns early at analog-engine.ts:307 and most error exits return without recording at line 660). Could not confirm error-vs-non-record split without runtime state capture (harness runs its own deck, not this uic programmatic build).

fixHint: Trace why _params.firstStep (resolveSimulationParams) is 0 for these transformer transient runs so the first-step dt feeds a nonzero ag0; the inductor/mutual stamp code is ngspice-faithful and is not the bug. Engine first-transient-step dt seeding for branch-equation reactive elements is the fix site.
diagnosis: harness_run on the tapped-transformer ac-sinusoid fixture: our engine produces only 1 step then errors Our engine did not advance at step 0 (simTime=0 lastDt=0) with 49 NR iterations vs ngspice 1009 steps avg 2.26 iters. harness_matrix_diff classification value-only: every coupled-inductor branch self-term and mutual cross-term is 0 on our side while ngspice has the L*ag0 companion (-200000 self, -160000/-80000 mutual) at step0/iter0. Cause: the first transient dt is 0 so ag[0]=0, making inductor req=ni.geq=ag0*L=0 (inductor.ts:871) and mutual stamps -mutFactor*ag0=0 (mutual-inductor.ts:236-238); the branch-equation diagonal -req=0 leaves the coupled-inductor matrix degenerate and NR stagnates. Confirmed NOT tapped-specific: the plain transformer-canon-ac-step-down fixture stagnates identically. _transientDcop seeds currentDt=_params.firstStep (analog-engine.ts:1091); firstStep is resolving/seeding to 0 for inductor-branch transformer topologies.

fixHint: Make setComponentProperty fan out the param to ALL analog sub-elements whose CircuitElement is the target (iterate elementToCircuitElement collecting every idx for the element, call setParam on each), so composite-nested consumers like the per-bit thresholders receive vIH.
diagnosis: setComponentProperty routes a parent hot-load to only ONE flattened analog sub-element. _resolveElementIndex (coordinator.ts:712-719) builds a Map keyed by CircuitElement, so for a composite all sub-elements collapse to the same parent key and only the last-written index survives. The DAC vIH must reach 8 DigitalInputThresholder leaves (two composite levels deep via DigitalInputPinLoaded) but the single setParam call at line 915-918 never fans out, so vIH stays at default 2.0 and 3.3V still reads HIGH (V_OUT stays ~2.5 instead of 0).

fixHint: Stamp a Thevenin output (target voltage behind rOut) in DACDriver.load instead of an ideal VSRC branch, and wire the rOut param into the driver so output impedance creates the divider; make rOut hot-loadable via setParam.
diagnosis: DACDriver stamps an ideal VSRC branch (V_OUT - V_GND = target exactly, lines 196-200) with no series output resistance, and buildDacNetlist (dac.ts) never wires the rOut param into any element. So the output is a stiff voltage source that cannot sag under load: V_OUT stays at 5.0 instead of 5*1e6/(1e6+1e3)=4.9950. The vIH/rOut hot-load also cannot take effect because DACDriver.setParam is a no-op (dac-driver.ts:159) and rOut is not consumed anywhere.

fixHint: In setParam, when key===rOut update this._rOut (or make load/G read this._p.rOut directly so the live value drives the conductance). The cached _rOut field and _p must not diverge.
diagnosis: OpampElement.setParam writes only this._p[key], but load() computes the RES conductance G = 1/this._rOut using the separate constructor-cached field this._rOut (opamp.ts:194,258) which setParam never updates. Hot-loading rOut from 100 to 4000 mutates _p.rOut but the stamp keeps using stale _rOut=100, so V(out) stays at 9.0909 instead of changing to 2.0.

fixHint: The discharge BJT must saturate and pull DIS toward GND when the base is driven to vDrop. Investigate the bjtDis NpnBJT spice-model area/IS defaults and the disBase clamp interaction (latch-driver.ts:123-126 G_base=1 Norton) so collector current actually flows; the base-clamp-only scheme produces a base voltage but no collector conduction.
diagnosis: Verified via circuit_dc_op: t:DIS stays pinned at ~5V (VCC) regardless of disBase. disBase correctly tracks vDrop at build time (fresh build vDrop=3 gives node disBase=3.0V, vDrop=1.5 gives 1.5V) yet t:DIS is 5.0V in both cases because the discharge BJT (bjtDis, NpnBJT spice model) sinks ~0 collector current even at Vbe=3V, so DIS is decoupled from the base and the test sees only 1.5e-9 NR-reconverge noise. Separately the hot-load patch path left disBase frozen at 1.5 (setParam routing not reaching latchDrv), but the dominant failure is the non-conducting discharge transistor.

fixHint: Implement setParam so key transconductance (and gain/m) rebuilds the linear expression / effectiveGm = transconductance*m used in stampOutput, mirroring how other controlled sources hot-reload; update the cached expression+derivative used by stampOutput.
diagnosis: VCCSAnalogElement.setParam is an empty no-op (params underscored, no body), so setComponentProperty(transconductance, 0.02) is silently dropped and V(out+) stays at -1V instead of -2V. The element drives current from a parsed expression / cached gm built at construction; setParam never updates it.

fixHint: In setParam case cIn forward the value to the child cap: this._capChild?.setParam(capacitance, value) (and reconcile when _capChild is null but value becomes >0). The spec field alone is not what gets stamped.
diagnosis: setParam(cIn) updates only this._spec.cIn but never the child AnalogCapacitorElement which holds its own capacitance baked at construction (line 62) and is what load() actually stamps (line 100-102). So hot-loading cIn=1e-7 does nothing; the RC keeps its default 1e-12 tau=1us and V(In_1) rises to ~2V at 5us instead of staying below 0.01V.

fixHint: Seed prevClock from the actual initial clock level so a pre-existing high does not register as a rising edge (e.g. initialize prevClock=clock on the first sample, or have the engine prime prevClock from initial inputs before the first executeFn). Edge must require a genuine low-to-high transition.
diagnosis: prevClock state slot initializes to 0. When C is set high before any step, the first sampleMonoflop sees clock=1 and prevClock=0 and the rising-edge test (clock!==0 && prevClock===0) fires a spurious trigger, so Q=1 instead of 0. There is no real 0->1 transition; the static high clock is misread as an edge because prevClock was never seeded with the initial clock level.

fixHint: Mask the packed output to the data bus width: read dataBits via layout.getProperty(index, dataBits) and AND the result with ((1<<dataBits)-1) (handle dataBits>=32 as full width) before writing state[wt[outputIdx]].
diagnosis: executeGraphicCard packs the D output (addr<<0 | str<<16 | clk<<17 | ld<<18 | bank<<19 = 983045 = 0xF0005) into the output slot with no mask to the dataBits bus width. dataBits=8 must truncate to the low byte (0xFF -> 5) but the function never reads dataBits and emits the full packed word, so D reads 983045 instead of 5.
